commands.review_summary:
The branch preserves the latest assistant thinking blocks during invalid-signature stripping, adds a retry path that drops thinking on Anthropic-style thinking failures, updates focused sanitizer tests, and adds a changelog entry.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main strips invalid thinking blocks from the most recent assistant turn in `stripInvalidThinkingSignatures`, and the replay pipeline applies that sanitizer before provider conversion. I did not run a live Anthropic credentialed replay in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Needs contributor real-behavior proof or maintainer override plus protected-label handling; there is no narrow automated code repair indicated by this review.

target.title: fix(agents): preserve latest thinking replay signatures
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #85557.

`stripInvalidThinkingSignatures` now preserves the latest assistant message while continuing to strip invalid thinking signatures from older replay turns. That matches the existing `dropThinkingBlocks` invariant and Anthropic's latest-turn replay contract for thinking blocks.

Updated the focused thinking tests and `sanitizeSessionHistory` coverage so older malformed turns still normalize, while latest assistant `thinking` and `redacted_thinking` content remains unchanged.

## Verification

- `corepack pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/thinking.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner.sanitize-session-history.test.ts src/agents/transcript-policy.test.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `corepack pnpm format:check CHANGELOG.md src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/thinking.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/thinking.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner.sanitize-session-history.test.ts`
- Blacksmith Testbox through Crabbox: `tbx_01ks98yhe3gk7j84tbkwbsbrhx`, Actions run https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26320476876, command `corepack pnpm check:changed`, exit 0

Autoreview note: local autoreview was attempted with `/mnt/c/src/claws-hapi/.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local --fallback-reviewer none`, but nested Codex review failed before review with OpenAI API 401 Unauthorized. I manually reviewed the touched code path and used the Testbox changed gate above for broad validation.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Anthropic Claude replay no longer mutates the latest assistant `thinking` or `redacted_thinking` blocks when invalid-signature stripping runs, avoiding provider rejections that require the latest assistant thinking content to be replayed unchanged.

Real environment tested: WSL2 OpenClaw worktree plus Blacksmith Testbox through Crabbox on Linux.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/thinking.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner.sanitize-session-history.test.ts src/agents/transcript-policy.test.ts`; `corepack pnpm check:changed` on Testbox `tbx_01ks98yhe3gk7j84tbkwbsbrhx`.

Evidence after fix: focused tests passed 5 files / 226 tests; Testbox changed gate passed core typecheck, core test typecheck, oxlint, import cycles, runtime sidecar, changelog, dependency, and guard lanes.

Observed result after fix: older assistant turns with missing or blank thinking signatures are still stripped or replaced with the omitted-reasoning fallback, while the latest assistant turn is preserved unchanged through `stripInvalidThinkingSignatures` and `sanitizeSessionHistory`.

What was not tested: live Anthropic API replay with real Claude credentials; the proof is source-level plus local and Testbox sanitizer coverage.
